steps.title: Connect curves to biophilic design
steps.content: Biophilic design connects buildings with nature and uses organic or biomorphic forms and textures. The word "therefore" signals that curves are common because they fit that nature-based design logic.

steps.title: Keep the nature connection
steps.content: Choice B only says curves are common across cultures, not why they suit biophilic design. Choice C introduces practicality, which the passage does not discuss. Choice D contradicts the goal of connecting architecture with nature. The needed completion explains that curves resemble forms found in living things. The answer is A.
